why_no_admission: The largest source-prevalence slice is JS/TS Promise async/scheduling at 29,094 occurrences, but scheduling, exception, aggregate-result, rejection-channel, and callback-effect obligations are still not all represented for exact convergence.
Callback-shaped surfaces are cross-language and measurable, but #599 only adds reporting; callback identity, result role, and effect visibility must be producer-backed before admission.

Channel/scheduling-shaped surfaces total 95,805 source-prevalence occurrences, but #600 intentionally keeps Promise/Future/async/channel/throws-like constructs closed until channel shape and scheduling proof exist.
#598 records hard negatives and known gaps, including aggregate first/settled behavior and cancellation/early-exit boundaries.
follow_up_entry_criteria_for_first_admis: A candidate must link #598 hard-negative rows for its false-merge modes.
A candidate must carry dependency-backed receiver/symbol/domain/call-target/library-api evidence.
A candidate must expose callback demand/effect or channel/scheduling obligations in by_obligation before and after.
A candidate must include positive fixtures, adjacent hard negatives, recall-loss deltas, product query-regression evidence, and runtime measurement.
closed_boundaries_left_explicit: Promise.all/race/allSettled aggregate result and first-settled behavior
thenable assimilation and unsafe Promise.resolve object values
Java Future/Stream and Swift async/throws convergence
C pointer/lifetime and non-local jump semantics
callback cardinality and effect visibility when callback identity is not proven
